Detailed Description

Macro Definition Documentation

◆ BTU2_BTU_FS_BTU_IP_REVISION_btu_ip_rev

#define BTU2_BTU_FS_BTU_IP_REVISION_btu_ip_rev (   x)

btu_ip_rev - BTU IP Revision, Bits[15:8] = major revision number (incremented at full tapeout), Bits[7:0] = minor revision number (incremented at post tapeout ECO), 88W8787 (Robin) = 0x0800 88W8790 (uBB) = 0x0800 88W8797 (Starling) = 0x900 88W8766 (Geagle) = 0x0900 88W8897 (Kfisher) = 0x1100 88W8777 (Robin2) = 0x1200 88W8887 (CardiAC) = 0x1300 8830 (Sunbird/BTHM) = 0x1400 88W8977 (Robin3/BTU2) = 0x1600 88W8997 (Kfisher2/BTU2) = 0x1700 88W8987 (Cardi2) = 0x1700 88W9064 (SCBT/BTU2) = 0x2100 88W9098 (Skyhawk/BTU2) = 0x2200 88W9000 (Kestrel/BTU2) = 0x2400 88W8978 (Robin3P/BTU2) = 0x2500 Firecrest/BTU2 = 0x2700 K4W1 (NBU/BTU2) = 0x2800 Redfinch/BLE_only = 0x2900 Owl/BTU2 = 0x2900 Tasman/BLE_only = 0x3000 Kamet/BTU2 =0x3100 Blackbird/BTU2 = 0x3200 KW47 (NBU/BTU2) = 0x3400 Hood/BTU2 = 0x3500 Fuji (NBU/BTU2) = 0x3600 MW30 (NBU/BTU2) = 0x3700

◆ BTU2_BTU_ERR_PAD_DATA_AES_CLK_CNT_MODE_err_pad_data

#define BTU2_BTU_ERR_PAD_DATA_AES_CLK_CNT_MODE_err_pad_data (   x)

err_pad_data - Padding Data for Erroneous Data Reporting

◆ BTU2_BTU_ERR_PAD_DATA_AES_CLK_CNT_MODE_aes_clk_cnt_mode

#define BTU2_BTU_ERR_PAD_DATA_AES_CLK_CNT_MODE_aes_clk_cnt_mode (   x)

aes_clk_cnt_mode - AES Clock Counter Mode Selection, 0x0: Using clock at the packet start time, 0x1: Master: Rx CLK uses Tx slot CLK; Slave: Tx CLK uses Rx slot CLK, 0x2: Master: ESCO_RETRANS Rx CLK = ESCO_RETRANS Tx slot CLK = ESCO_INSTANT Tx slot, CLK; Slave: ESCO_RETRANS Tx CLK = ESCO_RETRANS Rx slot CLK = ESCO_INSTANT Rx, slot CLK, 0x3: Master: ESCO_RETRANS Tx CLK uses ESCO_INSTANT Tx slot CLK; Slave: ESCO_RETRANS, Rx CLK uses ESCO_INSTANT Rx slot CLK

◆ BTU2_BTU_ERR_PAD_DATA_AES_CLK_CNT_MODE_corruptmic

#define BTU2_BTU_ERR_PAD_DATA_AES_CLK_CNT_MODE_corruptmic (   x)

corruptmic - corrupt MIC value on AES packet, 0x0: No corruption of the MIC value, 0x1: Enable corrupt MIC value on the packet

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_CTL0_brf_rd_data_turnaround

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_CTL0_brf_rd_data_turnaround (   x)

brf_rd_data_turnaround - BRF Read Data Turnaround, 0x0: BRF read data will be ready on the next clock cycle after address given, 0x1: BRF needs one more clock cycle between address and read data

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_CTL0_brf_agc_counter_readback_enable

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_CTL0_brf_agc_counter_readback_enable (   x)

brf_agc_counter_readback_enable - Enable BRF readback LNA; CBPF and ADC overflow counters after AGC freeze, 0x0: BRF only readback RSSI and noise floor values at RSSI, 0x1: BRF readback extra 3 bytes (LNA; CBPF and ADC overflow counters) after RSSI read

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_CTL0_radio_type

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_CTL0_radio_type (   x)

radio_type - Radio Type, 0x0: Unknown, 0x1: Marvell Gen3 radio; dBus mode, 0x2: Marvell Gen4 radio; dBus mode

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_CTL0_radio_mode

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_CTL0_radio_mode (   x)

radio_mode - Radio Mode Selection, 0x0: Marvell Radio Mode, 0x1: KW Radio; Sideband RIF interface, 0x2: Unused, 0x3: Unused

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BRF_RSSI_ADDR_rssi_addr

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BRF_RSSI_ADDR_rssi_addr (   x)

rssi_addr - RSSI Address, Address of the RSSI register in the radio.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_CTRL1_bt_reg_rif_bt_4m_clk_ena

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_CTRL1_bt_reg_rif_bt_4m_clk_ena (   x)

bt_reg_rif_bt_4m_clk_ena - RIF Clock Gate Enable Override, RIF Clock Gate Enable Override; if set to 1; clock gating for RIF clock is disabled = ungated clocks.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_CTRL1_bd_spi_data_bidir

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_CTRL1_bd_spi_data_bidir (   x)

bd_spi_data_bidir - Uni- or Bi-directional dBus Data Line Selection, 0x0: uni-directional, 0x1: bi-directional

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_CTRL1_bd_rxtx_data_bidir

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_CTRL1_bd_rxtx_data_bidir (   x)

bd_rxtx_data_bidir - Uni- or Bi-directional RxData and TxData Line Selection, 0x0: uni-directional, 0x1: bi-directional

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_CTRL1_rssi_value

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_CTRL1_rssi_value (   x)

rssi_value - RSSI Value, The latest received RSSI value from the radio. This can be used to get RSSI values before a link is setup; for example; for FCS. To get RSSI value for an existing link use the DIAG area in EBRAM.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_RXON_rxon_fixed

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_RXON_rxon_fixed (   x)

rxon_fixed - RxOn fixed or use programmable timing, 0x0: use start and stop timing parameters, 0x1: rxOn = RxOn_Start[6]

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_RXON_rxon_stop

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_RXON_rxon_stop (   x)

rxon_stop - Delay from Rx finished (rifRx low) to rxOn falling edge, - Resolution: 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_PLLCLKEN_ON_DELAY_brf_pll_clk_en_on_delay_qus

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_PLLCLKEN_ON_DELAY_brf_pll_clk_en_on_delay_qus (   x)

brf_pll_clk_en_on_delay_qus - Delay from wakeUp to turning of BRF's PLL clock, - Resolution: 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_PLLCLKEN_ON_DELAY_brf_pll_clk_early_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_PLLCLKEN_ON_DELAY_brf_pll_clk_early_en (   x)

brf_pll_clk_early_en - Activate brf_pll_clk_en earlier along with brf_pu, 0x0: Activate brf_pll_clk_en at normal delay, 0x1: Activate brf_pll_clk_en earlier along with brf_pu

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_PLLCLKEN_ON_DELAY_brf_pll_clk_en_nb

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_PLLCLKEN_ON_DELAY_brf_pll_clk_en_nb (   x)

brf_pll_clk_en_nb - Activate brf_pll_clk_en during NB/MB scan packets Enable, 0x0: keep off during NB/MB scan packets, 0x1: activate brf_pll_clk_en during NB/MB scan packets

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_PLLCLKEN_ON_DELAY_brf_pll_clk_en_fixed

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_PLLCLKEN_ON_DELAY_brf_pll_clk_en_fixed (   x)

brf_pll_clk_en_fixed - Use fixed value for brf_pll_clk_en, 0x0: on/off_delay registers control the timing, 0x1: brf_pll_clk_en is the fixed value of bit[0]

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_TXON_txon_fixed

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_TXON_txon_fixed (   x)

txon_fixed - TxOn fixed; or use programmable timing, 0x0: use start and stop timing parameters, 0x1: txOn = TxOn_Start[6]

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_TXON_txon_stop

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_TXON_txon_stop (   x)

txon_stop - Delay from Tx finished (rifTx low) to txOn falling edge, - Resolution: 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_PLLCLKEN_OFF_DELAY_brf_pll_clk_en_off_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_PLLCLKEN_OFF_DELAY_brf_pll_clk_en_off_delay (   x)

brf_pll_clk_en_off_delay - Delay from final dBus access finishing to turning off BRF PLL clock, - Resolution: 0.25 us.

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_FREQ_rxtx_freq (   x)

rxtx_freq - Rx or Tx Slot Indication, 0x0: Tx slot, 0x1: Rx slot

freq - Channel Hop Frequency Number

brf_tx_pu_on_delay_qus - brf_pu on delay, Delay from wakeUp to turning on brf_pu. Resolution: 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BRF_TX_PU_brf_tx_pu_off_delay_qus

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BRF_TX_PU_brf_tx_pu_off_delay_qus (   x)

brf_tx_pu_off_delay_qus - brf_pu off delay, Delay from last dBus access to turning off brf_pu. Resolution: 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BRF_TX_PU_brf_tx_pu_fixed

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BRF_TX_PU_brf_tx_pu_fixed (   x)

brf_tx_pu_fixed - Set brf_pu to a fixed value., BRF power up value., 0x0: use programmable timers, 0x1: fixed; brf_pu = brf_tx_pu_on_delay_us[0]

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_CTRL2_tx_data_inv

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_CTRL2_tx_data_inv (   x)

tx_data_inv - Invert Tx data, 0x0: normal operation, 0x1: transmit data inverted

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_CTRL2_rx_data_inv

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_CTRL2_rx_data_inv (   x)

rx_data_inv - Invert Rx data, 0x0: normal operation, 0x1: receive data inverted

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_TX_PULSE_tx_pulse_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_TX_PULSE_tx_pulse_delay (   x)

tx_pulse_delay - Tx Pulse Delay, Delay between wakeUp and txData sync plulse for dBus radio types. Resolution 0.25 us. The following formula must be fulfilled: doTxPulse < TxOn_Start + LinkDelay.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_TX_PULSE_tx_pulse_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_TX_PULSE_tx_pulse_en (   x)

tx_pulse_en - Enable TxData sync pulse, 0x0: TxData sync pulse deactivated and pulse not generated, 0x1: TxData sync pulse activated and pulse generated

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_TX_PWRCTL_dbus_tx_pwr_ctrl_wr_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_TX_PWRCTL_dbus_tx_pwr_ctrl_wr_delay (   x)

dbus_tx_pwr_ctrl_wr_delay - Delay from the trigger to start Tx Power Control Write dBus, Delay from rif_tx_req to enable Tx power control dBus writes. Resolution: 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_TX_PWRCTL_dbus_tx_pwr_ctrl_wr_enable

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_TX_PWRCTL_dbus_tx_pwr_ctrl_wr_enable (   x)

dbus_tx_pwr_ctrl_wr_enable - Enable Automatic Tx Power Control Write on the dBus

dbus_rx_pwr_ctrl_wr_delay - Delay from the trigger to start Rx Power Control Write dBus, Delay from rif_rx_req to enable Rx power control dBus writes. Resolution: 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_RX_PWRCTL_dbus_rx_pwr_ctrl_wr_enable

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_RX_PWRCTL_dbus_rx_pwr_ctrl_wr_enable (   x)

dbus_rx_pwr_ctrl_wr_enable - Enable Automatic Rx Power Control Write on dBus

rx_pwr_ctrl_addr - Radio Register Address for Rx Power Control

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BRF_FSYN_ADDR_fsyn_addr (   x)

fsyn_addr - Radio Register Start Address for Fsyn data

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BRF_TXPWRCTL_ADDR_tx_pwr_ctrl_addr (   x)

tx_pwr_ctrl_addr - Radio Register Address for Tx Power Control

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BTC_DBUS_RSSI_dbus_rssi_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BTC_DBUS_RSSI_dbus_rssi_delay (   x)

dbus_rssi_delay - Delay from RSSI trigger to start of RSSI read, Delay from the end of rif_rx_req to enable dBus RSSI read back., Resolution 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BTC_DBUS_RSSI_dbus_rssi_enable

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BTC_DBUS_RSSI_dbus_rssi_enable (   x)

dbus_rssi_enable - Enable Automatic Reading of RSSI on the dBus, 0x0: disable automatic reading of RSSI, 0x1: enable automatic reading of RSSI

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_TX_FSYN_dbus_tx_fsyn_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_TX_FSYN_dbus_tx_fsyn_delay (   x)

dbus_tx_fsyn_delay - Tx dBus Fsyn Delay, Delay from start of new Tx slot (i.e.; rif_tx_req) to sending the fsync information on the dBus., Resolution 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_TX_FSYN_dbus_tx_fsyn_wr_enable

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_TX_FSYN_dbus_tx_fsyn_wr_enable (   x)

dbus_tx_fsyn_wr_enable - Tx dBus Fsyn Enable, Enables Tx automatic sending of fsync information on the dBus., 0x0: disable automatic sending of fsync info, 0x1: enable automatic sending of fsync info

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_RX_FSYN_dbus_rx_fsyn_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_RX_FSYN_dbus_rx_fsyn_delay (   x)

dbus_rx_fsyn_delay - Rx dBus Hop Delay, Delay time after start of Rx new slot (i.e.; rif_rx_req) to sending the fsync information on the dBus., Resolution 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_RX_FSYN_dbus_rx_fsyn_wr_enable

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_RX_FSYN_dbus_rx_fsyn_wr_enable (   x)

dbus_rx_fsyn_wr_enable - Rx dBus Fsyn Enable, Enable Rx automatic sending of fsync information on the dBus., 0x0: disable automatic sending of fsync info, 0x1: enable automatic sending of fsync info

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_RX_PKT_LEN_dbus_rx_cte_len_enable

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_RX_PKT_LEN_dbus_rx_cte_len_enable (   x)

dbus_rx_cte_len_enable - dBus Rx CTE Length Enable, Enable Automatic Sending Rx Packet CTE Length on the dBus., 0x0: disable automatic dbus sending Rx packet CTE length, 0x1: enable automatic dbus sending Rx packet CTE length

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_RX_PKT_LEN_dbus_rx_pkt_len_enable

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_RX_PKT_LEN_dbus_rx_pkt_len_enable (   x)

dbus_rx_pkt_len_enable - dBus Rx Packet Length Enable, Enable Automatic Sending Rx Packet Payload Length (including payload header; data; and crc) on the dBus., 0x0: disable automatic dbus sending Rx packet payload length, 0x1: enable automatic dbus sending Rx packet payload length

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_PULSE_RX_DELAY_ble_pulse_rx_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_PULSE_RX_DELAY_ble_pulse_rx_delay (   x)

ble_pulse_rx_delay - BLE Pulse Rx Delay, Timing Variable from rif_rx_req to BLE Pulse on Rx. Only assert under BLE traffic; and must be less or equal to BTU_RIF_RXPAON[9:0] - 4., - Resolution: 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_PULSE_TX_DELAY_ble_pulse_tx_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_PULSE_TX_DELAY_ble_pulse_tx_delay (   x)

ble_pulse_tx_delay - BLE Pulse Tx Delay, Timing Variable from rif_rx_req to BLE Pulse on Tx. Only assert under BLE traffic; and must be less or equal to BTU_RIF_TXPAON[9:0] - 4., - Resolution: 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_RX_MODE_TYPE_dbus_rx_mode_type_enable

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_RX_MODE_TYPE_dbus_rx_mode_type_enable (   x)

dbus_rx_mode_type_enable - dBus Rx Mode Type Enable, Enable Automatic Sending Rx Mode Type (decoded from packet header) on the dBus., 0x0: disable automatic dbus sending Rx mode type, 0x1: enable automatic dbus sending Rx mode type

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_MODE_TYPE_TX_DELAY_mode_type_tx_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_MODE_TYPE_TX_DELAY_mode_type_tx_delay (   x)

mode_type_tx_delay - Mode Type Tx Delay, Time from the assertion of modTypeTx to the deassertion of modTypeTx., - Resolution: 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_MODE_TYPE_RX_DELAY_mode_type_rx_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_MODE_TYPE_RX_DELAY_mode_type_rx_delay (   x)

mode_type_rx_delay - Mode Type Rx Delay, Time from the assertion of modTypeRx to the deassertion of modTypeRx. For Gen4; it anchors the end of packet header for Rx sampling., - Resolution: 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_PKTCTL_OE_DELAY_pkt_ctl_oe_delay_modetype

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_PKTCTL_OE_DELAY_pkt_ctl_oe_delay_modetype (   x)

pkt_ctl_oe_delay_modetype - Packet Control OE Delay Mode Type, Time from the Deassertion of modType to the Deassertion of pktCtl_oe., - Resolution: 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_PKTCTL_OE_DELAY_pkt_ctl_oe_delay_ppulse

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_PKTCTL_OE_DELAY_pkt_ctl_oe_delay_ppulse (   x)

pkt_ctl_oe_delay_ppulse - Packet Control OE Delay P Pulse, Time from the Deassertion of P pulse to the Deasserttion of pktCtl oe., - Resolution: 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_PKTCTL_OE_DELAY_pkt_ctl_oe_delay_hdrstart

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_PKTCTL_OE_DELAY_pkt_ctl_oe_delay_hdrstart (   x)

pkt_ctl_oe_delay_hdrstart - Packet Control OE Delay Header Start, Time from the Deassertion of Header Start pulse to the Assertion of pktCtl oe., - Resolution: 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_PKTCTL_OE_DELAY_rf_bypass

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_PKTCTL_OE_DELAY_rf_bypass (   x)

rf_bypass - Radio Bypass Mode Used for Baseband to Baseband Testing, When set; it will do two things on pktCtrl_1:, - On Rx slot; it sets output enable as input., - On Tx slot; it generates a sync pulse before payload.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_CTL_sw_dbus_addr

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_CTL_sw_dbus_addr (   x)

sw_dbus_addr - dBus register address, BRF register offset address.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_CTL_sw_dbus_size_bytes

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_CTL_sw_dbus_size_bytes (   x)

sw_dbus_size_bytes - number of bytes to read or write, Burst read or write is supported; a maximum of 8 bytes at once., 0x0: 1 byte, 0x1: 2 bytes, 0x2: 3 bytes, 0x3: 4 bytes, 0x4: 5 byteS, 0x5: 6 bytes, 0x6: 7 bytes, 0x7: 8 bytes

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_CTL_sw_dbus_start

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_CTL_sw_dbus_start (   x)

sw_dbus_start - Start a new dBus access, A new dBus register access is started when 1 is written to this bit (provided an access is not currently in progress). The read value is always 0.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_CTL_sw_dbus_rd

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_CTL_sw_dbus_rd (   x)

sw_dbus_rd - Read/write control, 0x0: write, 0x1: read

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_EVENT_sw_dbus_count

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_EVENT_sw_dbus_count (   x)

sw_dbus_count - Count to trigger software dBus, Count value used in conjunction with sw_dbus_event; when applicable., - Resolution: 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_EVENT_sw_dbus_rst

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_EVENT_sw_dbus_rst (   x)

sw_dbus_rst - software dBus reset, If set; software dBus FSM will reset and transition to idle state. Software dBus finish will be issued. Firmware first writes 1 and then writes 0 to this bit.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_EVENT_sw_dbus_finished

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_EVENT_sw_dbus_finished (   x)

sw_dbus_finished - Poll status of dBus access, When set; the dBus read or write access is finished. This bit is reset when writing to BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_CTL reg for the next access., 0x0: busy, 0x1: finished

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_EVENT_sw_dbus_event

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_EVENT_sw_dbus_event (   x)

sw_dbus_event - Event to trigger software dBus access, SWDBUS_EVENT_NONPKT,0x0: SWDBUS_EVENT_NONPKT (when there is no packet activity), SWDBUS_EVENT_PKT_ASAP,0x1: SWDBUS_EVENT_PKT_ASAP (as soon as possible during packet activity), SWDBUS_EVENT_PKT_START,0x2: SWDBUS_EVENT_PKT_START (when rif_startCount equals sw_dbus_count), SWDBUS_EVENT_PKT_END,0x3: SWDBUS_EVENT_PKT_END (when rif_endCount equals sw_dbus_count)

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_DATA_1_0_sw_dbus_wdata0

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_DATA_1_0_sw_dbus_wdata0 (   x)

sw_dbus_wdata0 - Software Access Data, Set write data (write access); or get the read data (read access)., Updated automatically during read., Bits[7:0] of 1st 8-bit word.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_DATA_1_0_sw_dbus_wdata1

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_DATA_1_0_sw_dbus_wdata1 (   x)

sw_dbus_wdata1 - Software Access Data, Set write data (write access); or get the read data (read access)., Updated automatically during read., Bits[7:0] of 2nd 8-bit word.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_DATA_3_2_sw_dbus_wdata2

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_DATA_3_2_sw_dbus_wdata2 (   x)

sw_dbus_wdata2 - Software Access Data, Set write data (write access); or get the read data (read access)., Updated automatically during read., Bits[7:0] of 3rd 8-bit word.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_DATA_3_2_sw_dbus_wdata3

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_DATA_3_2_sw_dbus_wdata3 (   x)

sw_dbus_wdata3 - Software Access Data, Set write data (write access); or get the read data (read access)., Updated automatically during read., Bits[7:0] of 4th 8-bit word.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_DATA_5_4_sw_dbus_wdata4

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_DATA_5_4_sw_dbus_wdata4 (   x)

sw_dbus_wdata4 - Software Access Data, Set write data (write access); or get the read data (read access)., Updated automatically during read., Bits[7:0] of 5th 8-bit word.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_DATA_5_4_sw_dbus_wdata5

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_DATA_5_4_sw_dbus_wdata5 (   x)

sw_dbus_wdata5 - Software Access Data, Set write data (write access); or get the read data (read access)., Updated automatically during read., Bits[7:0] of 6th 8-bit word.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_DATA_7_6_sw_dbus_wdata6

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_DATA_7_6_sw_dbus_wdata6 (   x)

sw_dbus_wdata6 - Software Access Data, Set write data (write access); or get the read data (read access)., Updated automatically during read., Bits[7:0] of 7th 8-bit word.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_DATA_7_6_sw_dbus_wdata7

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_SWDBUS_DATA_7_6_sw_dbus_wdata7 (   x)

sw_dbus_wdata7 - Software Access Data, Set write data (write access); or get the read data (read access)., Updated automatically during read., Bits[7:0] of 8th 8-bit word.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_TX_FESW_tx_fesw_on_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_TX_FESW_tx_fesw_on_delay (   x)

tx_fesw_on_delay - Tx FESW On Delay, Number of microseconds from wakeUp to turning on bttx_fesw for Tx packets.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_TX_FESW_tx_fesw_off_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_TX_FESW_tx_fesw_off_delay (   x)

tx_fesw_off_delay - Tx FESW Off Delay, Number of microseconds from rifTx falling to turning off bttx_fesw.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_TX_FESW_tx_fesw_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_TX_FESW_tx_fesw_en (   x)

tx_fesw_en - Tx Front-End Switch (FESW) Enable, 0x0: bttx_fesw is fixed to 0, 0x1: enable FESW during for Tx packets

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_FESW_rx_fesw_on_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_FESW_rx_fesw_on_delay (   x)

rx_fesw_on_delay - Rx FESW On Delay, Number of microseconds from rif_rx_req to turning on btrx_fesw for Rx packets.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_FESW_rx_fesw_off_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_FESW_rx_fesw_off_delay (   x)

rx_fesw_off_delay - Rx FESW Off Delay, Number of microseconds from rifRx falling to turning off btrx_fesw.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_FESW_rx_fesw_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_FESW_rx_fesw_en (   x)

rx_fesw_en - Rx FESW Enable, 0x0: btrx_fesw is fixed to 0, 0x1: enable FESW during for Rx packets

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_RXPAON_rx_pa_on_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_RXPAON_rx_pa_on_delay (   x)

rx_pa_on_delay - Rx PA on Delay, Timing Variable from Wakeup (packet boundary) to PA Assertion on Rx Pulse., - Resolution: 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_RXPAON_rx_pa_on_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_RXPAON_rx_pa_on_en (   x)

rx_pa_on_en - Rx PA On Delay Enable

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BRF_RX_PU_brf_rx_pu_on_delay_qus

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BRF_RX_PU_brf_rx_pu_on_delay_qus (   x)

brf_rx_pu_on_delay_qus - brf_pu on delay, Delay from wakeUp to turning on brf_pu. Resolution: 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BRF_RX_PU_brf_rx_pu_off_delay_qus

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BRF_RX_PU_brf_rx_pu_off_delay_qus (   x)

brf_rx_pu_off_delay_qus - brf_pu off delay, Delay from last dBus access to turning off brf_pu. Resolution: 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BRF_RX_PU_brf_rx_pu_fixed

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BRF_RX_PU_brf_rx_pu_fixed (   x)

brf_rx_pu_fixed - Set brf_pu to a fixed value., BRF power up value., 0x0: use programmable timers, 0x1: fixed; brf_pu = brf_pu_on_delay_us[0]

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BTC_DBUS_TX_PKT_END_dbus_tx_pkt_end_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BTC_DBUS_TX_PKT_END_dbus_tx_pkt_end_delay (   x)

dbus_tx_pkt_end_delay - dBus Tx Packet End Delay, Time from the end of active Tx payload to dBus command that sets the radio back to IDLE., - Resolution: 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BTC_DBUS_TX_PKT_END_dbus_tx_pkt_end_enable

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BTC_DBUS_TX_PKT_END_dbus_tx_pkt_end_enable (   x)

dbus_tx_pkt_end_enable - Tx Packet End dBus Transaction Enable

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BTC_DBUS_RX_PKT_END_dbus_rx_pkt_end_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BTC_DBUS_RX_PKT_END_dbus_rx_pkt_end_delay (   x)

dbus_rx_pkt_end_delay - dBus Rx Packet End Delay, Time from the end of active Rx payload to dBus command that sets the radio back to IDLE., - Resolution: 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BTC_DBUS_RX_PKT_END_dbus_rx_pkt_end_enable

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BTC_DBUS_RX_PKT_END_dbus_rx_pkt_end_enable (   x)

dbus_rx_pkt_end_enable - Rx Packet End dBus Transaction Enable

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BTC_DBUS_TX_RAMPDOWN_dbus_tx_rampdown_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BTC_DBUS_TX_RAMPDOWN_dbus_tx_rampdown_delay (   x)

dbus_tx_rampdown_delay - dBus Tx Rampdown Delay, Delay time after the end of a Tx slot for ramping down the radio over the dBus., Resolution 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BTC_DBUS_TX_RAMPDOWN_dbus_tx_rampdown_enable

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BTC_DBUS_TX_RAMPDOWN_dbus_tx_rampdown_enable (   x)

dbus_tx_rampdown_enable - Enables Automatic Rampdown radio after Tx slot on the dBus, 0x0: disable automatic ramping down the radio after Tx slot, 0x1: enable automatic ramping down the radio after Tx slot

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_COR_CONFIG_CTRL_dbus_corcfg_wr_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_COR_CONFIG_CTRL_dbus_corcfg_wr_delay (   x)

dbus_corcfg_wr_delay - Rx Mode dBus BRF Correlator Configuration Control Delay, Delay from rif_rx_req to trigger correlator configuration dBus writes., - Resolution: 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_COR_CONFIG_CTRL_cor_prem_det_gate_syncwd_en_ant

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_COR_CONFIG_CTRL_cor_prem_det_gate_syncwd_en_ant (   x)

cor_prem_det_gate_syncwd_en_ant - Enable BRF to start syncword detection after preamble found for Ant Mode

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_COR_CONFIG_CTRL_cor_prem_det_gate_syncwd_en_nike

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_COR_CONFIG_CTRL_cor_prem_det_gate_syncwd_en_nike (   x)

cor_prem_det_gate_syncwd_en_nike - Enable BRF to start syncword detection after preamble found for Nike Mode

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_COR_CONFIG_CTRL_cor_prem_det_gate_syncwd_en_ble

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_COR_CONFIG_CTRL_cor_prem_det_gate_syncwd_en_ble (   x)

cor_prem_det_gate_syncwd_en_ble - Enable BRF to start syncword detection after preamble found for BLE Mode

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_COR_CONFIG_CTRL_dbus_corcfg_wr_enable

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_COR_CONFIG_CTRL_dbus_corcfg_wr_enable (   x)

dbus_corcfg_wr_enable - Rx Mode dBus BRF Correlator Configuration Transaction Enable, Enable only when using MRVL Gen3 radio.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BRF_COR_CONFIG_ADDR_rx_cor_cfg_addr

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BRF_COR_CONFIG_ADDR_rx_cor_cfg_addr (   x)

rx_cor_cfg_addr - Radio Register Address for Correlator Configuration, Only use in MRVL Gen3 radio mode., The BRF register of the correlator configuration.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_SYNCWD_ACC_CODE_CTRL_dbus_syncword_wr_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_SYNCWD_ACC_CODE_CTRL_dbus_syncword_wr_delay (   x)

dbus_syncword_wr_delay - dBus Syncword and Access Code Control Delay, Delay from rif_rx_req to trigger syncword and access code control dBus writes., - Resolution: 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_SYNCWD_ACC_CODE_CTRL_dbus_syncword_wr_enable

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_SYNCWD_ACC_CODE_CTRL_dbus_syncword_wr_enable (   x)

dbus_syncword_wr_enable - Rx Mode dBus Syncword and Access Code Transaction Enable

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BRF_ACC_CODE_ONE_ADDR_rx_acc_code_one_addr

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BRF_ACC_CODE_ONE_ADDR_rx_acc_code_one_addr (   x)

rx_acc_code_one_addr - Radio Register Address for First Access Code, The LSB BRF register of the 1st access code. Since BTC sync word is 64-bit long; the consecutive 8 BRF registers (1 byte each register) are allocated. Correlation hit used this sync word will reflect a PKT_CTL_0 pulse.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BRF_ACC_CODE_TWO_ADDR_rx_acc_code_two_addr

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BRF_ACC_CODE_TWO_ADDR_rx_acc_code_two_addr (   x)

rx_acc_code_two_addr - Radio Register Address for Second Access Code, The LSB BRF register of the 2nd access code. Since BTC sync word is 64-bit long; the consecutive 8 BRF registers (1 byte each register) are allocated. Correlation hit used this sync word will reflect a PKT_CTL_1 pulse.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BRF_RX_MODE_TYPE_ADDR_rx_mode_type_addr

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BRF_RX_MODE_TYPE_ADDR_rx_mode_type_addr (   x)

rx_mode_type_addr - Radio Register Address for Rx Mode Type, The BRF register of the expected Rx BDR/EDR mode type.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BRF_RX_PKT_PLD_LEN_ADDR_rx_pkt_pld_len_addr

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BRF_RX_PKT_PLD_LEN_ADDR_rx_pkt_pld_len_addr (   x)

rx_pkt_pld_len_addr - Radio Register Address for Rx Packet Payload Length, The LSB BRF register of the expected Rx Packet Payload Length. The immediate next BRF registers is also allocated for MSB length portion., - Units: micro-seconds.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_SYNC_PULSE_TIMER_rx_hdr_pulse_timeout

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_SYNC_PULSE_TIMER_rx_hdr_pulse_timeout (   x)

rx_hdr_pulse_timeout - Rx Header Pulse Timeout, After correlation hit; timeout counter for the header sync pulse from the BRF. The BRF header start pulse is received when the packet header is sent on rx_data. If the header start pulse is not detected; then the packet would be aborted. This timeout specifies how long the BTU will wait before an abort., - Resolution: 1 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_SYNC_PULSE_TIMER_rx_sync_pulse_timeout

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_SYNC_PULSE_TIMER_rx_sync_pulse_timeout (   x)

rx_sync_pulse_timeout - Rx Sync Pulse Timeout, After header start pulse; timeout counter for the payload sync pulse from the BRF. The BRF payload sync pulse is received when the packet payload is sent on rx_data. If the payload sync pulse is not detected; then the packet would be aborted. This timeout specifies how long the BTU will wait before an abort., - Resolution: 1 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_BTC_SAMP_PHASE_ADJ_hdr_start_pos_adj

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_BTC_SAMP_PHASE_ADJ_hdr_start_pos_adj (   x)

hdr_start_pos_adj - Positive Phase Adjustment after Header starts Pulse, Positive phase adjustment after header starts pulse. The sampling point is delayed by the shift amount., - Resolution: 0.25 us (Gen3); 62.5 ns (Gen4).

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_BTC_SAMP_PHASE_ADJ_hdr_start_neg_adj

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_BTC_SAMP_PHASE_ADJ_hdr_start_neg_adj (   x)

hdr_start_neg_adj - Negative Phase Adjustment after Header starts Pulse, Negative phase adjustment after header starts pulse. The sampling point is advanced by the shift amount., - Resolution: 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_BTC_SAMP_PHASE_ADJ_sync_start_pos_adj

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_BTC_SAMP_PHASE_ADJ_sync_start_pos_adj (   x)

sync_start_pos_adj - Positive Phase Adjustment after Payload Sync starts Pulse, Positive phase adjustment after payload sync starts pulse. The sampling point is delayed by the shift amount.,

  • Resolution: 0.25 us (Gen3); 62.5 ns (Gen4).

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_BTC_SAMP_PHASE_ADJ_sync_start_neg_adj

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_BTC_SAMP_PHASE_ADJ_sync_start_neg_adj (   x)

sync_start_neg_adj - Negative Phase Adjustment after Header Starts Pulse, Negative phase adjustment after payload sync starts pulse. The sampling point is advanced by the shift amount., - Resolution: 0.25 us (Gen3); 62.5 ns (Gen4).

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_BLE_SAMP_PHASE_ADJ_pdu_start_pos_adj

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_BLE_SAMP_PHASE_ADJ_pdu_start_pos_adj (   x)

pdu_start_pos_adj - Positive Phase Adjustment after PDU Data starts Pulse, Positive phase adjustment after PDU data starts pulse. The sampling point is delayed by the shift amount., - Resolution: 0.25 us (Gen3); 62.5 ns (Gen4).

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_BLE_SAMP_PHASE_ADJ_pdu_start_neg_adj

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_BLE_SAMP_PHASE_ADJ_pdu_start_neg_adj (   x)

pdu_start_neg_adj - Negative Phase Adjustment after PDU Data starts Pulse, Negative phase adjustment after PDU data starts pulse. The sampling point is advanced by the shift amount., - Resolution: 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_BLE_SAMP_PHASE_ADJ_dfe_1m_phase_pos_adj

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_BLE_SAMP_PHASE_ADJ_dfe_1m_phase_pos_adj (   x)

dfe_1m_phase_pos_adj - Positive Phase Adjustment for DFE I/Q Samples After CRC, Positive Phase Adjustment for 1 Mbps DFE I/Q samples after CRC. The sampling point is delayed by the shift amount., - Resolution: 62.5 ns (Gen4).

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_BLE_SAMP_PHASE_ADJ_dfe_1m_phase_neg_adj

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_BLE_SAMP_PHASE_ADJ_dfe_1m_phase_neg_adj (   x)

dfe_1m_phase_neg_adj - Negative Phase Adjustment for DFE I/Q Samples After CRC, Positive Phase Adjustment for 1 Mbps DFE I/Q samples after CRC. The sampling point is advanced by the shift amount., - Resolution: 62.5 ns (Gen4).

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_BLE_SAMP_PHASE_ADJ_dfe_2m_phase_pos_adj

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_BLE_SAMP_PHASE_ADJ_dfe_2m_phase_pos_adj (   x)

dfe_2m_phase_pos_adj - Positive Phase Adjustment for DFE I/Q Samples After CRC, Positive Phase Adjustment for 2 Mbps DFE I/Q samples after CRC. The sampling point is delayed by the shift amount., - Resolution: 62.5 ns (Gen4).

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_BLE_SAMP_PHASE_ADJ_dfe_2m_phase_neg_adj

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_BLE_SAMP_PHASE_ADJ_dfe_2m_phase_neg_adj (   x)

dfe_2m_phase_neg_adj - Negative Phase Adjustment for DFE I/Q Samples After CRC, Positive Phase Adjustment for 2 Mbps DFE I/Q samples after CRC. The sampling point is advanced by the shift amount., - Resolution: 62.5 ns (Gen4).

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_TONE_CANCEL_CTRL_rx_dbus_pull_in_time

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_TONE_CANCEL_CTRL_rx_dbus_pull_in_time (   x)

rx_dbus_pull_in_time - Rx dBUS events pull-in time, This adjusts the pull-in time of the dBUS events including SOC_BT_BRF_PU; A3; A1; A2 and P pulse before Rx packet. This setting applies to both BTC and BLE packet. Resolution: 0.25us

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_TONE_CANCEL_CTRL_ble_pull_in_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_TONE_CANCEL_CTRL_ble_pull_in_en (   x)

ble_pull_in_en - BLE dBUS events pull-in enable, Enable BLE Rx packet dBUS events pull-in when current BLE packet's frequency matches one of the 4 TONE_CANCEL_CHNL values., 0x0: disable, 0x1: enable

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_TONE_CANCEL_CHNL10_tone_cancel_chnl0

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_TONE_CANCEL_CHNL10_tone_cancel_chnl0 (   x)

tone_cancel_chnl0 - Rx Tone Cancellation Channel 0, If Rx packet's channel index value sent to BRF on A2 event matches this value; the Rx packet dBUS event will be pulled-in; upon enabled.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_TONE_CANCEL_CHNL10_tone_cancel_chnl1

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_TONE_CANCEL_CHNL10_tone_cancel_chnl1 (   x)

tone_cancel_chnl1 - Rx Tone Cancellation Channel 1, If Rx packet's channel index value sent to BRF on A2 event matches this value; the Rx packet dBUS event will be pulled-in; upon enabled.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_TONE_CANCEL_CHNL32_tone_cancel_chnl2

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_TONE_CANCEL_CHNL32_tone_cancel_chnl2 (   x)

tone_cancel_chnl2 - Rx Tone Cancellation Channel 2, If Rx packet's channel index value sent to BRF on A2 event matches this value; the Rx packet dBUS event will be pulled-in; upon enabled.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_TONE_CANCEL_CHNL32_tone_cancel_chnl3

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_RX_TONE_CANCEL_CHNL32_tone_cancel_chnl3 (   x)

tone_cancel_chnl3 - Rx Tone Cancellation Channel 3, If Rx packet's channel index value sent to BRF on A2 event matches this value; the Rx packet dBUS event will be pulled-in; upon enabled.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CTL_ble_brf_pu

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CTL_ble_brf_pu (   x)

ble_brf_pu - BLE BRF Power up, This bit used by the BLE software to control the power up bit for the BRF., 0x0: disable, 0x1: enable

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CTL_ble_pll_req

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CTL_ble_pll_req (   x)

ble_pll_req - BLE PLL Clock Request, This bit is used by the BLE software to control the PLL clock enable., 0x0: disable, 0x1: enable

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CTL_data_loopback_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CTL_data_loopback_en (   x)

data_loopback_en - BLE Loopback Data Enable, Setting this bit changes the source of the BLE Rx data from the Tx block instead of from the BRF., 0x0: Rx data is from BRF, 0x1: Rx data is from Tx block

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CTL_rx_iq_sample_phase

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CTL_rx_iq_sample_phase (   x)

rx_iq_sample_phase - BLE Rx IQ Sampling Rate Phase, To use in conjunction with rx_iq_sample_rate[1:0] in 'BLE_REG_DFE_PKT_EXTENSION_CNTRL'; define IQ sample reception phase in Rx extension field., When rx_iq_sample_rate[1:0] == 0; receiving IQ samples every 4 usec; on the phase rx_iq_sample_phase[1:0] herein specified (i.e. 0~3)., When rx_iq_sample_rate[1:0] == 1; receiving IQ samples every 2 usec; on the phase rx_iq_sample_phase[0] herein specified (i.e. 0: on even phase; 1: on odd phase)., When rx_iq_sample_rate[1:0] == 0; receiving IQ samples every 1 usec on every phase; rx_iq_sample_phase[1:0] is ignored.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CTL_ble_tx_pwr_adj_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CTL_ble_tx_pwr_adj_en (   x)

ble_tx_pwr_adj_en - BLE TX power adjust enable, Global enable for BLE Tx power adjustment scheme

  • outputting Tx_power_level to BCA at bt_req and overwriting with Bfdbk_Tx_power if Bfdbk_Tx_power_ind., 0x0: disable, 0x1: enable

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CTL_ble_ignore_bca_tx_pwr

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CTL_ble_ignore_bca_tx_pwr (   x)

ble_ignore_bca_tx_pwr - BLE ignore BCA TX power, FW overide to ignore BCA's Bfdbk_Tx_power if Bfdbk_Tx_power_ind; for current BLE Tx pkt., 0x0: disable, 0x1: enable

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_COR_LENGTH_SEL_ble_cor_indicator

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_COR_LENGTH_SEL_ble_cor_indicator (   x)

ble_cor_indicator - BLE Correlator Indicator, BLE correlator indicator to enable which correlator is used., 0x0: no correlator enabled, 0x1: correlator one enabled, 0x2: correlator two enabled, 0x3: both correlators enabled

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_COR_ACCESSADDR_0_ble_access_code_0_16

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_COR_ACCESSADDR_0_ble_access_code_0_16 (   x)

ble_access_code_0_16 - BLE Access Code, BLE Access Code to be used in correlator for the current Rx packet (lower 16-bit).

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_COR_ACCESSADDR_1_ble_access_code_17_32

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_COR_ACCESSADDR_1_ble_access_code_17_32 (   x)

ble_access_code_17_32 - BLE Access Code, BLE Access Code to be used in correlator for the current Rx packet(upper 16-bit).

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_COR_WINDOW_0_ble_cor_window_0

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_COR_WINDOW_0_ble_cor_window_0 (   x)

ble_cor_window_0 - BLE Correlation Window Size, BLE correlation window size for the current Rx packet (lower 16-bit)., Correlation window in 1/4 usec. Hardware will automatically abort Rx operation up expiration of this window time. Maximum window size: 2^26/4 = 16777216 usec.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_COR_WINDOW_1_ble_cor_window_1

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_COR_WINDOW_1_ble_cor_window_1 (   x)

ble_cor_window_1 - BLE Correlation Window Size, BLE correlation window size for the current Rx packet (Upper 10-bit)., Correlation window in 1/4 usec. Hardware will automatically abort Rx operation up expiration of this window time. Maximum window size: 2^26/4 = 16777216 usec.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_EN_DELAY_tx_clk_en_delay_qus

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_EN_DELAY_tx_clk_en_delay_qus (   x)

tx_clk_en_delay_qus - Tx_clk_en Delay Offset, This is the delay offset for the tx_clk_en signal from the Tx S pulse., - Resolution: 1/4 usec.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_EN_DELAY_rx_clk_en_delay_qus

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_EN_DELAY_rx_clk_en_delay_qus (   x)

rx_clk_en_delay_qus - Rx_clk_en Delay Offset, This is the delay offset for the rx_clk_en signal from the Rx P pulse., - Resolution: 1/4 usec.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_GO_DELAY_rif_tx_go_delay_qus

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_GO_DELAY_rif_tx_go_delay_qus (   x)

rif_tx_go_delay_qus - RIF_TX_GO Delay Offset, This is the delay offset for the rif_tx_go signal from the Tx S pulse., - Res: 1/4 usec.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_GO_DELAY_rif_rx_go_delay_qus

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_GO_DELAY_rif_rx_go_delay_qus (   x)

rif_rx_go_delay_qus - RIF_RX_GO Delay Offset, This is the delay offset for the rif_rx_go signal from the Rx P pulse., - Res: 1/4 usec.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_DBUS_TX_PWRCTL_WDATA_rif_dbus_tx_pwrctl_wdata

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_DBUS_TX_PWRCTL_WDATA_rif_dbus_tx_pwrctl_wdata (   x)

rif_dbus_tx_pwrctl_wdata - Tx Power Control DBUS Write Data, Tx A1 pulse., - Bits[9:8]: Tx data rate, - Bits[7:0]: Tx power table index, 0x0: reserved, 0x1: BDR 1 Mbps / BLE 1 Mbps / ANT 1 Mbps, 0x2: EDR 2 Mbps / BLE 2 Mbps, 0x3: EDR 3 Mbps

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_DBUS_TX_FSYN_WDATA_rif_dbus_tx_fsyn_wdata

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_DBUS_TX_FSYN_WDATA_rif_dbus_tx_fsyn_wdata (   x)

rif_dbus_tx_fsyn_wdata - Tx Fsyn DBUS Write Data, Tx A2 pulse., - Bits[15:13]: Bluetooth mode - BTC; BLE; NIKE; ANT or LLR, - Bit[12]: Rx notch filter enable, - Bit[11]: reserved, - Bit[10]: power up BRF BG/CPREG, - Bits[9:8]: Bluetooth state - Tx; Rx, - Bit[7]: large correlation window indicator, - Bits[6:0]: Bluetooth channel from 0 to 78

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_DBUS_RX_PWRCTL_WDATA_rif_dbus_rx_pwrctl_wdata

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_DBUS_RX_PWRCTL_WDATA_rif_dbus_rx_pwrctl_wdata (   x)

rif_dbus_rx_pwrctl_wdata - Rx Power Control DBUS Write Data, Rx A1 pulse., - Bits[15:14]: reserved, - Bit[13]: BLE MI estimation enable, - Bit[11]: Rx link BDR/EDR packet type, - Bit[10]: Rx channel estimation using noise floor, - Bits[9:8]: indicate which syncword correlator to use,

  • Bits[7:4]: Rx link index from 0 to 9, - Bits[1:0]: Rx power mode (when low power scan mode is normal mode; selects from either normal or performance mode), ,Enum, - Bit[12]: BLE Rx data rate, 0x0: 1 Mbps (also for BTC; ANT), 0x1: 2 Mbps, ,Enum, - Bits[2]: BLE channel assessment mode, 0x0: Single channel, 0x1: V_1, Multiple channels (ch; ch+1; ch+2 ... ch+9)

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_DBUS_RX_FSYN_WDATA_rif_dbus_rx_fsyn_wdata

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_DBUS_RX_FSYN_WDATA_rif_dbus_rx_fsyn_wdata (   x)

rif_dbus_rx_fsyn_wdata - Rx Fsyn DBUS Write Data, Rx A2 pulse., - Bits[15:13]: Bluetooth mode - BTC; BLE; NIKE; ANT or LLR, - Bit[12]: Rx notch filter enable, - Bit[11]: reserved, - Bit[10]: power up BRF BG/CPREG, - Bits[9:8]: Bluetooth state - Tx; Rx, - Bit[7]: large correlation window indicator, - Bit[6:0]: Bluetooth channel from 0 to 78

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_RX_SYNC_DELAY_ble_rx_syncpulse_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_RX_SYNC_DELAY_ble_rx_syncpulse_delay (   x)

ble_rx_syncpulse_delay - The BLE packet PDU sync pulse max delay from correlation hit pulse. Watchdog timer value for sync pulse from BRF at the start of PDU. Hardware will automatically abort Rx operation upon expiration of the watchdog timer. Res: 1 usec.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_RX_SYNC_DELAY_ble_rx_datavld_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_RX_SYNC_DELAY_ble_rx_datavld_delay (   x)

ble_rx_datavld_delay - In Rx bursting mode; the BLE packet PDU bursting data valid max delay from last end of data valid or sync pulse. Watchdog timer value for sync pulse from BRF at the start of PDU. Hardware will automatically abort Rx operation upon expiration of the watchdog timer. Res: 1 usec.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_RX_PKT_STATUS_ble_tx_pwr_adjd

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_RX_PKT_STATUS_ble_tx_pwr_adjd (   x)

ble_tx_pwr_adjd - BLE Tx power adjusted status, This bit indicates that BLE TX power was adjusted by BCA feedback. This bit is cleared on read. It is valid shortly after BCA grant is received; for each BLE TX packet.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_RX_PKT_STATUS_rf_bt_test_mode_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_RX_PKT_STATUS_rf_bt_test_mode_en (   x)

rf_bt_test_mode_en - Enable BRF Bluetooth Test Mode

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_RX_PKT_STATUS_bfdbk_tx_power_4m_ble_rdbk

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_RX_PKT_STATUS_bfdbk_tx_power_4m_ble_rdbk (   x)

bfdbk_tx_power_4m_ble_rdbk - BCA Feedback power for BLE packet, Feedback power value received from BCA for BLE TX packet. This value is overwritten when new BLE power level override is feedback from BCA.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_RX_MODETYPE_rf_rx_mode_type_resrv

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_RX_MODETYPE_rf_rx_mode_type_resrv (   x)

rf_rx_mode_type_resrv - reserved register

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BRF_RX_CTE_LEN_ADDR_rx_cte_len_addr

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BRF_RX_CTE_LEN_ADDR_rx_cte_len_addr (   x)

rx_cte_len_addr - Radio Register Address for Rx CTE Length, The BRF register of the expected Rx CTE length., - Units: 8 micro-seconds.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_ARB_BLE_START_TIME_CODED_ble_tx_start_time

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_ARB_BLE_START_TIME_CODED_ble_tx_start_time (   x)

ble_tx_start_time - Delay when bt_grant_n is sampled and rif_tx_req will start after start_rif_tx (Tx Request from BLE to RIF-ARB for radio access) is issued from BLE., - Resolution: 1/4 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_ARB_BLE_START_TIME_CODED_ble_rx_start_time

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_ARB_BLE_START_TIME_CODED_ble_rx_start_time (   x)

ble_rx_start_time - Delay when bt_grant_n is sampled and rif_rx_req will start after start_rif_rx (Rx request from BLE to RIF-ARB for Radio access) is issued., - Resolution : 1/4 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_ARB_BLE_START_TIME_1M_ble_tx_start_time

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_ARB_BLE_START_TIME_1M_ble_tx_start_time (   x)

ble_tx_start_time - Delay when bt_grant_n is sampled and rif_tx_req will start after start_rif_tx (Tx Request from BLE to RIF-ARB for radio access) is issued from BLE., - Resolution: 1/4 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_ARB_BLE_START_TIME_1M_ble_rx_start_time

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_ARB_BLE_START_TIME_1M_ble_rx_start_time (   x)

ble_rx_start_time - Delay when bt_grant_n is sampled and rif_rx_req will start after start_rif_rx (Rx request from BLE to RIF-ARB for Radio access) is issued., - Resolution : 1/4 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_ARB_BLE_START_TIME_2M_ble_tx_start_time

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_ARB_BLE_START_TIME_2M_ble_tx_start_time (   x)

ble_tx_start_time - Delay when bt_grant_n is sampled and rif_tx_req will start after start_rif_tx (Tx Request from BLE to RIF-ARB for radio access) is issued from BLE., - Resolution: 1/4 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_ARB_BLE_START_TIME_2M_ble_rx_start_time

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_ARB_BLE_START_TIME_2M_ble_rx_start_time (   x)

ble_rx_start_time - Delay when bt_grant_n is sampled and rif_rx_req will start after start_rif_rx (Rx request from BLE to RIF-ARB for Radio access) is issued., - Resolution : 1/4 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_ARB_CTL_ble_sw_abort

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_ARB_CTL_ble_sw_abort (   x)

ble_sw_abort - If set; current BLE traffic will be aborted and ble_sw_abort_intr will be issued., This bit cleared by hardware; after ble_sw_abort_intr is issued., If there is no ongoing BLE traffic; then there is no abort happening. But ble_sw_abort_intr will be issued anyway. It is software requirement to issue ble_sw_abort_intr weather there was ongoing BLE traffic got aborted or not.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_ARB_CTL_rst_rif_arb_fsm

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_ARB_CTL_rst_rif_arb_fsm (   x)

rst_rif_arb_fsm - RIF-ARB Reset, If set; RIF-ARB fsm will reset and transition to idle state. Firmware first writes 1 and then writes 0 to this bit.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_ARB_CTL_ble_sync_frame_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_ARB_CTL_ble_sync_frame_en (   x)

ble_sync_frame_en - Enable ble_sync_frame in arbitration decision, Enable considering ble_sync_frame in arbitration decision, 0x0: disable, 0x1: enable

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_ARB_CTL_rif_collide

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_ARB_CTL_rif_collide (   x)

rif_collide - KW47 Deadlock condition detection; with interrupt generation on ble_cutoff, This bit is set if there is a rising edge on rif_tx or rx during an on-going transaction. This bit gets cleared with a WtiteOne

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_CCA_GRANT_OFFSET_TIME_ble_cca_grant_offset_time

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_CCA_GRANT_OFFSET_TIME_ble_cca_grant_offset_time (   x)

ble_cca_grant_offset_time - BLE CCA Grant Offset time, Offset to the delay when bt_grant_n is sampled and rif_tx_start will start after rifTx (Tx request from BLE to RIF-ARB for radio access) is issued from BLE. This offset is only used in TX packets; and when ble_tx_cca_en is also set to 1., - Resolution: 0.25us

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_CCA_TX_FESW_OFFSET_TIME_ble_cca_tx_fesw_offset_time

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_CCA_TX_FESW_OFFSET_TIME_ble_cca_tx_fesw_offset_time (   x)

ble_cca_tx_fesw_offset_time - BLE CCA TX FESW Offset time, Offset to the delay assertion of bttx_fesw in CCA mode to curb during CCA (RX) operation is ongoing. This offset is added on to tx_fesw_on_delay. This offset is only used in BLE TX packets; and when ble_tx_cca_en is also set to 1., - Resolution: 1us

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_TESTBUS_SEL_0_btu_testbus_sel_0

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_TESTBUS_SEL_0_btu_testbus_sel_0 (   x)

btu_testbus_sel_0 - RIF Testbus Select; Lower Bits, Lower 15 bits of btu_testbus_sel; if, (btu_testbus_sel[i*2];btu_testbus_sel[i*2+1]) set to:, 0x0: get btc_testbus_out[i], 0x1: get ble_testbus_out[i], 0x2: get ble_debug_sw_msg_out(URAT), 0x3: get ble_test_sw_out[i]

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_TESTBUS_SEL_1_btu_testbus_sel_1

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_TESTBUS_SEL_1_btu_testbus_sel_1 (   x)

btu_testbus_sel_1 - RIF Testbus Select; Upper Bits, Upper 15 bits of btu_testbus_sel; if, (btu_testbus_sel[i*2];btu_testbus_sel[i*2+1]) set to:, 0x0: get btc_testbus_out[i], 0x1: get ble_testbus_out[i], 0x2: get ble_debug_sw_msg_out(URAT), 0x3: get ble_test_sw_out[i]

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_TESTBUS_OE_btu_testbus_oe_

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_TESTBUS_OE_btu_testbus_oe_ (   x)

btu_testbus_oe_ - Debug testbus output enable, Active low BTU testbus output enables.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_REG_DEBUG_SW_OUTDATA_testbus_sw_out

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_REG_DEBUG_SW_OUTDATA_testbus_sw_out (   x)

testbus_sw_out - BLE debug software control testbus ouput, Only when btu_testbus_overrdie_by_ble_sw = 1; this software testbus is the BLE testbus output.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_REG_DEBUG_INDATA_testbus_in

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_REG_DEBUG_INDATA_testbus_in (   x)

testbus_in - BLE debug testbus input

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_REG_DEBUG_SW_MSG_debug_sw_msg

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_REG_DEBUG_SW_MSG_debug_sw_msg (   x)

debug_sw_msg - Debug message, Software message is written into this register and transfered into FIFO.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_REG_DEBUG_SW_MSG_FIFO_CNT_ble_reg_debug_sw_msg_fifo_cnt

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_REG_DEBUG_SW_MSG_FIFO_CNT_ble_reg_debug_sw_msg_fifo_cnt (   x)

ble_reg_debug_sw_msg_fifo_cnt - Debug message FIFO count

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_TESTBUS_CONTROL_btu_rif_testbus_control

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_TESTBUS_CONTROL_btu_rif_testbus_control (   x)

btu_rif_testbus_control - Testbus bank rotation

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_TESTBUS_CONTROL_btu_rif_testbus_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_TESTBUS_CONTROL_btu_rif_testbus_en (   x)

btu_rif_testbus_en - Enable debug mode to use BTU testbus

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BT_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_aes_clk_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BT_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_aes_clk_en (   x)

bt_reg_aes_clk_en - AES Clock Enable, If set; Force bt_aes_clk to turn on., Otherwise; bt_aes_clk_en controls bt_aes_clk gating.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BT_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_rif_rx_4m_clk_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BT_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_rif_rx_4m_clk_en (   x)

bt_reg_rif_rx_4m_clk_en - RIF Rx 4M Clock Enable, 0x0: rif_rx_4m_clk_en controls clock gating, 0x1: forces rif_rx_4m_clk to turn on

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BT_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_rif_rx_16m_clk_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BT_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_rif_rx_16m_clk_en (   x)

bt_reg_rif_rx_16m_clk_en - RIF Rx 16M Clock Enable, 0x0: rif_rx_16m_clk_en controls clock gating for rif_rx_16m_clk, 0x1: forces rif_rx_16m_clk to turn on

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BT_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_force_ebram_sysclk_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BT_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_force_ebram_sysclk_en (   x)

bt_reg_force_ebram_sysclk_en - Force EBRAM ebSysClk enable, When set; ebSysClk to EBRAM is always ON; it is not gated.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BT_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_aes_sys_clk_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BT_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_aes_sys_clk_en (   x)

bt_reg_aes_sys_clk_en - Force aes sys clk enable, When set; sys clk to hw aes arbiter is always ON; it is not gated.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_CLK_SMPLD_btu_4m_smpld_mux

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_CLK_SMPLD_btu_4m_smpld_mux (   x)

btu_4m_smpld_mux - 4M Sampled MUX, Mux select to select the sync method., First method does not require the clocks to be in phase., Second wethod requires cloks to be in phase (synchronization between 4 MHz clock and ebSysClkIn)., If this bit is set to 1; it uses first method., This signal is used for both BTC and BLE.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_CLK_SMPLD_rif_rx_4m_smpld_mux

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_CLK_SMPLD_rif_rx_4m_smpld_mux (   x)

rif_rx_4m_smpld_mux - RIF Rx 4M Sampled MUX, Mux select to select sync methods., First method does not require clocks to be in phase., Second method requires clocks to be in phase (Used in synchronization between 16 MHz and 4 MHz clock. Used for sampling Gen4 data.), If set to 1; select first method.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_ble_sleep_clk_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_ble_sleep_clk_en (   x)

bt_reg_ble_sleep_clk_en - BLE Sleep Clock Enable, If set; forces ble_sleep_clk to turn on.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_ble_force_clk_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_ble_force_clk_en (   x)

bt_reg_ble_force_clk_en - BLE Force Clock Enable, If set; forces all BLE internal clocks to turn on.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_ble_slv_hclk_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_ble_slv_hclk_en (   x)

bt_reg_ble_slv_hclk_en - BLE Slave HCLK Enable, 0x0: ble_slv_hclk_en controls clock gating dynamically, 0x1: forces ble_sys_td_clk_en to turn on

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_ble_mst_hclk_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_ble_mst_hclk_en (   x)

bt_reg_ble_mst_hclk_en - BLE Master HCLK Enable, 0x0: ble_mst_hclk_en controls clock gating, 0x1: forces ble_mst_hclk to 1

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_ble_ric_sys_clk_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_ble_ric_sys_clk_en (   x)

bt_reg_ble_ric_sys_clk_en - BLE RIC System Clock Enable, 0x0: ble_ric_sys_clk_en controls dynamic clock gating, 0x1: force ble_ric_sys_clk to turn on

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_ble_tx_clk_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_ble_tx_clk_en (   x)

bt_reg_ble_tx_clk_en - BLE Tx Clock Enable, 0x0: ble_tx_clk_en controls clock gating for ble_tx_clk, 0x1: forces ble_tx_clk; also forces ble_td_sys_clk_en to be set

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_ble_rx_clk_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_ble_rx_clk_en (   x)

bt_reg_ble_rx_clk_en - BLE Rx Clock Enable, 0x0: ble_rx_clk controls clock gating dynamically, 0x1: forces ble_rx_clk clocks to turn on; also forces ble_rd_sys_clk to turn on

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_ble_td_sys_clk_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_ble_td_sys_clk_en (   x)

bt_reg_ble_td_sys_clk_en - BLE TD System Clock Enable, 0x0: ble_td_sys_clk_en controls clock gating dynamically, 0x1: forces ble_td_sys_clk to turn on

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_ble_rd_sys_clk_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_ble_rd_sys_clk_en (   x)

bt_reg_ble_rd_sys_clk_en - BLE RD System Clock Enable, 0x0: ble_rd_sys_clk_en controls dynamic clock gating, 0x1: forces ble_rd_sys_clk to turn on

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_ble_rl_sys_clk_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_ble_rl_sys_clk_en (   x)

bt_reg_ble_rl_sys_clk_en - BLE RD System Clock Enable, 0x0: ble_rl_sys_clk_en controls dynamic clock gating, 0x1: forces forces ble_rl_sys_clk to turn on

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_ble_wl_sys_clk_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_ble_wl_sys_clk_en (   x)

bt_reg_ble_wl_sys_clk_en - BLE Whitelist Search System Clock Enable, 0x0: ble_wl_sys_clk_en controls dynamic clock gating, 0x1: forces ble_wl_sys_clk to turn on

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_ble_irk_sys_clk_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_ble_irk_sys_clk_en (   x)

bt_reg_ble_irk_sys_clk_en - BLE IRK resolve System Clock Enable, 0x0: ble_irk_sys_clk_en controls dynamic clock gating, 0x1: forces ble_irk_sys_clk to turn on

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_ble_tmrs_clk_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_ble_tmrs_clk_en (   x)

bt_reg_ble_tmrs_clk_en - BLE cdt and pst timers clock enable, 0x0: ble_tmr_4m_clk_en controls dynamic clock gating, 0x1: forces ble_tmr_4m_clk_en to turn on

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_ble_tmrreg_sys_clk_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_GATING_bt_reg_ble_tmrreg_sys_clk_en (   x)

bt_reg_ble_tmrreg_sys_clk_en - BLE timer registers clock enable, 0x0: BTU controls dynamic sysclk gating on SWT; CDT and PST registers, 0x1: forces SWT; CDT and PST registers sysclk to turn on

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_CTRL_bt_reg_force_ble_clk_req_zero

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_CTRL_bt_reg_force_ble_clk_req_zero (   x)

bt_reg_force_ble_clk_req_zero - Force BLE Clock Req Zero, ble_sp_req is set to put BLE to sleep. When a write (1 or 0) to this register is detected; BLE will set ble_clk_req to 0 and go to sleep. This will gate off all BLE clocks.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_CTRL_bt_reg_force_ble_clk_req_one

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_CTRL_bt_reg_force_ble_clk_req_one (   x)

bt_reg_force_ble_clk_req_one - Force BLE Clock Req One, When set; ble_clk_req will always be forced to 1.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_CTRL_ble_awake_sts

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_CTRL_ble_awake_sts (   x)

ble_awake_sts - BLE Awake Status, This bit indicates if BLE is in sleep or awake.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_CTRL_ble_clk_req_early_assert

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_CTRL_ble_clk_req_early_assert (   x)

ble_clk_req_early_assert - ble_clk_req early assertion upon any BLE AHB access, Setting this bit will enable ble_clk_req early assertion upon any BLE AHB access; which allows bt_clk_req to be asserted earlier to APU.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_CTRL_ble_sys_4m_early_ungate

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CLK_CTRL_ble_sys_4m_early_ungate (   x)

ble_sys_4m_early_ungate - ble_sys_clk and ble_4m_clk early ungated upon any BLE AHB access, Setting this bit will allow ble_sys_clk and ble_4m_clk being ungated earlier upon any BLE AHB access during BLE sleep for faster BLE register access.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_DBUS_TX_PKT_END_dbus_tx_pkt_end_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_DBUS_TX_PKT_END_dbus_tx_pkt_end_delay (   x)

dbus_tx_pkt_end_delay - dBus Tx Packet End Delay, Time from the end of active Tx payload to dBus command that sets the radio back to IDLE., - Resolution: 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_DBUS_RX_PKT_END_dbus_rx_pkt_end_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_DBUS_RX_PKT_END_dbus_rx_pkt_end_delay (   x)

dbus_rx_pkt_end_delay - dBus Rx Packet End Delay, Time from the end of active Rx payload to dBus command that sets the radio back to IDLE., - Resolution: 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_DBUS_TX_RAMPDOWN_dbus_tx_rampdown_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_DBUS_TX_RAMPDOWN_dbus_tx_rampdown_delay (   x)

dbus_tx_rampdown_delay - dBus Tx Rampdown Delay, Delay time after the end of a Tx slot for ramping down the radio over the dBus., Resolution 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_DBUS_RSSI_dbus_rssi_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_DBUS_RSSI_dbus_rssi_delay (   x)

dbus_rssi_delay - Delay from RSSI trigger to start of RSSI read, Delay from the end of rif_rx_req to enable dBus RSSI read back., Resolution 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_ADAPTIVITY_CTRL_ble_cca_dis

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_ADAPTIVITY_CTRL_ble_cca_dis (   x)

ble_cca_dis - BLE CCA disable bit, FW override to disable CCA for current BLE Tx pkt. - Revert to normal Tx timing; clear A1 cca_before_tx_en bit; ignore brf_bt_cca_tx_drop; and no R2 DBUS. FW should set this bit before start_rif_tx time; and clear this bit before the next TX packet's start_rif_tx time.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_ADAPTIVITY_CTRL_cca_ed_factor

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_ADAPTIVITY_CTRL_cca_ed_factor (   x)

cca_ed_factor - Forgetfactor for CCA NF linear mean averaging, Controls how many previous NF readings in the same channel are considered for NF linear mean averaging

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_ADAPTIVITY_CTRL_ble_cca_ebram_rst_dyn

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_ADAPTIVITY_CTRL_ble_cca_ebram_rst_dyn (   x)

ble_cca_ebram_rst_dyn - Reset bit for BLE CCA abort counters, Reset all BLE CCA abort counters in EBRAM. This bit is self clearing.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_ADAPTIVITY_CTRL_ble_cca_ebram_stat_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_ADAPTIVITY_CTRL_ble_cca_ebram_stat_en (   x)

ble_cca_ebram_stat_en - Enable bit for BLE CCA EBRAM STATISTICS, Enable BLE CCA abort counters and NF averaging in EBRAM.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_ADAPTIVITY_CTRL_ble_tx_nf_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_ADAPTIVITY_CTRL_ble_tx_nf_en (   x)

ble_tx_nf_en - BLE NF during TX enable, Reading inband power in each TX channel gives the Noise Floor in that channel. This can be used for NF measurement; replacing scheduled NF measurement slots in BLE. When this bit is set; design works in NF mode. CCA is performed and NF is read back. brf_bt_cca_tx_drop indication from BRF is ignored. This bit is meant to be used together with ble_tx_cca_en bit ble_tx_cca_en = 1; ble_tx_nf_en = x:CCA is done; NF is read back; brf abort indication aborts TX (EU regulatory mode) ble_tx_cca_en = 0; ble_tx_nf_en = 1:CCA is done; NF is read back; brf abort indication ignored (Non EU countries can use this mode) ble_tx_cca_en = 0; ble_tx_nf_en = 0:CCA is not done before TX. Old behavior (legacy)

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_ADAPTIVITY_CTRL_ble_tx_cca_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_ADAPTIVITY_CTRL_ble_tx_cca_en (   x)

ble_tx_cca_en - BLE CCA before TX enable, Global enable for BLE CCA before Tx scheme - new CA1; CA2 to BRF; bt_pll_req; brf_pu; fesw; bca_bt_grant_n new Tx timing to BCA/BRF; CCA abort handling (to BCA); new R2 DBUS; cca abort statistic in EBRAM and NF averaged in EBRAM

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CCA_ED_READBACK_ble_cca_ed_mag

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CCA_ED_READBACK_ble_cca_ed_mag (   x)

ble_cca_ed_mag - BLE CCA ED Magnitude, BLE CCA ED Magnitude readback from R2 DBUS)

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CCA_ED_READBACK_ble_cca_ed_vld

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CCA_ED_READBACK_ble_cca_ed_vld (   x)

ble_cca_ed_vld - BLE CCA ED valid, BLE CCA ED valid indication. If packet is aborted before R2 time; the ble_cca_ed_mag may be invalid. This bit is set to 1 when ble_cca_ed_mag is valid.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_CCA_TX_PWRCTL_dbus_cca_tx_pwr_ctrl_wr_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_CCA_TX_PWRCTL_dbus_cca_tx_pwr_ctrl_wr_delay (   x)

dbus_cca_tx_pwr_ctrl_wr_delay - CCA TX PWRCTL Dbus Delay, Delay value to position CA1 timing. Resolution 0.25us

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_CCA_TX_PWRCTL_dbus_cca_tx_pwr_ctrl_wr_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_CCA_TX_PWRCTL_dbus_cca_tx_pwr_ctrl_wr_en (   x)

dbus_cca_tx_pwr_ctrl_wr_en - CCA TX PWRCTL Dbus Enable, Enable CA1 DBUS. CA1 is performed only when this bit is set and also BT adaptivity features is enabled for the current controller (BTC or BLE)

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_CCA_TX_FSYN_dbus_cca_tx_fsyn_wr_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_CCA_TX_FSYN_dbus_cca_tx_fsyn_wr_delay (   x)

dbus_cca_tx_fsyn_wr_delay - CCA TX FSYN Dbus Delay, Delay value to position CA1 timing. Resolution 0.25us

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_CCA_TX_FSYN_dbus_cca_tx_fsyn_wr_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_CCA_TX_FSYN_dbus_cca_tx_fsyn_wr_en (   x)

dbus_cca_tx_fsyn_wr_en - CCA TX FSYN Dbus Enable, Enable CA2 DBUS. CA2 is performed only when this bit is set and also BT adaptivity features is enabled for the current controller (BTC or BLE)

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_CCA_ED_dbus_cca_ed_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_CCA_ED_dbus_cca_ed_delay (   x)

dbus_cca_ed_delay - CCA TX R2 Dbus Delay, Delay value to position R2 timing. The delay is counted from the end of S pulse on BTU-BRF interface.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_CCA_ED_dbus_cca_ed_en

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_DBUS_CCA_ED_dbus_cca_ed_en (   x)

dbus_cca_ed_en - CCA TX R2 Dbus Enable, Enable R2 DBUS. R2 is performed only when this bit is set and also BT adaptivity features is enabled for the current controller (BTC or BLE)

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BRF_CCA_ED_ADDR_dbus_cca_ed_addr

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BRF_CCA_ED_ADDR_dbus_cca_ed_addr (   x)

dbus_cca_ed_addr - BRF CCA ED Address, BRF register address for Inband Power Readback via R2 DBUS.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BTC_CCA_CA1_2_DELAY_rif_ca1_2_offset_for_abort_b4_cca_us

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BTC_CCA_CA1_2_DELAY_rif_ca1_2_offset_for_abort_b4_cca_us (   x)

rif_ca1_2_offset_for_abort_b4_cca_us - CA1 CA2 offset for the case of abort before CCA TX, Additional offset to delay CA1 and CA2 when CCA pkt was after a bt rif abort or FFU bca abort. The default is to delay CA1;2 in these cases by 10us to allow BRF MCU to accept these DBUS timely.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CCA_EBRAM_ADDR_ble_cca_ebram_addr

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CCA_EBRAM_ADDR_ble_cca_ebram_addr (   x)

ble_cca_ebram_addr - BLE CCA EBRAM Address Offset, This register is the EBRAM offset value for BLE CCA statistics location.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CCA_THRESHOLD_rif_ble_cca_abort_pkt_cnt_thd

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CCA_THRESHOLD_rif_ble_cca_abort_pkt_cnt_thd (   x)

rif_ble_cca_abort_pkt_cnt_thd - BLE CCA abort packet count threshold, 8-bit value of threshold to be used along with EBRAM abort statistics. BTU will generate ble_cca_abort_thd_cnt_met interrupt to SW when the number of CCA aborted packets in any single channel have reached or exceeded this threshold value.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CCA_THRESHOLD_rif_ble_cca_tx_pkt_cnt_thd

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_BLE_CCA_THRESHOLD_rif_ble_cca_tx_pkt_cnt_thd (   x)

rif_ble_cca_tx_pkt_cnt_thd - BLE CCA TX packet count threshold, 8-bit value of threshold to be used along with EBRAM abort statistics. BTU will generate ble_cca_tx_cnt_thd_met interrupt to SW when the number of total CCA TX packets in any single channel have reached or exceeded this threshold value.

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_CCA_BLE_THRESHOLD_MET_STS_rif_ble_cca_abort_cnt_thd_met_ch

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_CCA_BLE_THRESHOLD_MET_STS_rif_ble_cca_abort_cnt_thd_met_ch (   x)

rif_ble_cca_abort_cnt_thd_met_ch - BLE CCA abort count threshold met channel number, BLE channel read-back value; on which the CCA abort threshold was met; causing ble_cca_abort_thd_met interrupt to CPU

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_CCA_BLE_THRESHOLD_MET_STS_rif_ble_cca_tx_cnt_thd_met_ch

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_CCA_BLE_THRESHOLD_MET_STS_rif_ble_cca_tx_cnt_thd_met_ch (   x)

rif_ble_cca_tx_cnt_thd_met_ch - BLE CCA TX count threshold met channel number, BLE channel read-back value; on which the CCA total TX packet count threshold was met; causing ble_cca_txpkt_thd_met interrupt to CPU

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_CCA_BLE_RX_FESW_DELAY_ble_btrx_fesw_during_cca_on_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_CCA_BLE_RX_FESW_DELAY_ble_btrx_fesw_during_cca_on_delay (   x)

ble_btrx_fesw_during_cca_on_delay - BLE btrx_fesw ON delay for CCA TX packets, Number of microseconds from wakeUp to turning on btrx_fesw for CCA portion of BLE Tx packets

◆ BTU2_BTU_RIF_CCA_BLE_RX_FESW_DELAY_ble_btrx_fesw_during_cca_off_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_RIF_CCA_BLE_RX_FESW_DELAY_ble_btrx_fesw_during_cca_off_delay (   x)

ble_btrx_fesw_during_cca_off_delay - BLE btrx_fesw OFF delay for CCA TX packets, Number of microseconds from wakeUp to turning off btrx_fesw for CCA portion of BLE Tx packets

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_CTRL_reg_bt_tx_pwr_gate_en

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_CTRL_reg_bt_tx_pwr_gate_en (   x)

reg_bt_tx_pwr_gate_en - BT tx power enable for BCA, BT tx power enable for BCA, 0x0: Bt tx power is disabled, 0x1: Bt tx power is enabled

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_CTRL_bt_reg_pta_rx_4m_clk_en

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_CTRL_bt_reg_pta_rx_4m_clk_en (   x)

bt_reg_pta_rx_4m_clk_en - PTA Clock Gate Enable Override, PTA Clock Gate Enable Override; if set to 1; clock gating for PTA clocks is disabled = ungated clocks.

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_CTRL_bt_grant_bypass_en

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_CTRL_bt_grant_bypass_en (   x)

bt_grant_bypass_en - BCA coex grant bypass enable, When bt_grant_bypass_en is set; BCA coex grant bca_bt_grant_n is controlled by bt_grant_bypass_val register., 0x0: disable bt_grant_n bypass, 0x1: enable bt_grant_n bypass

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_CTRL_bt_grant_bypass_val

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_CTRL_bt_grant_bypass_val (   x)

bt_grant_bypass_val - BCA coex grant bypass value, When bt_grant_bypass_en is set; BCA coex grant bca_bt_grant_n is controlled by this register., 0x0: set BCA coex grant bt_grant_n to zero, 0x1: set BCA coex grant bt_grant_n to one

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_CTRL_clralltxcntrs

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_CTRL_clralltxcntrs (   x)

clralltxcntrs - Clear All Tx Counters, When set; all of the PTA diagnostic counters are reset to 0:, - Tx_Req_Hi_Cnt, - Tx_Loss_Hi_Cnt, - Tx_Loss_Med_Cnt, - Tx_Req_Med_Cnt, - Tx_Req_Med_Hi; Cnt, - Tx_Loss_Med_Hi_Cnt, 0x0: do not clear counters, 0x1: clear counters

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_CTRL_mrvlcoexexiten

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_CTRL_mrvlcoexexiten (   x)

mrvlcoexexiten - Marvell Coexistence Exit Enable, Enable the reset of the MRVL coexistence state machine if neither rifTx nor rifRx is active at frameTick such that BT_REQ will go low., 0x0: disable, 0x1: enable

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_REQ_DELAY_TIMER_pta_req_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_REQ_DELAY_TIMER_pta_req_delay (   x)

pta_req_delay - PTA Request Delay, When BTU uses MRVL Coexistence mode; this controls the delay from pta_tx_req/pta_rx_req to the rising edge of the BT_REQ., This value should never be programmed more than 15us; otherwise unexpected behavior will occur; such as dropped packets due to BCA grant timeout., - Unit: 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_BLE_RIF_ARB_COEX_PRI_ble_tx_pri

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_BLE_RIF_ARB_COEX_PRI_ble_tx_pri (   x)

ble_tx_pri - BLE Coex Priority, The BLE Tx packet high/medium priority that is used to make RIF_ARB arbitration request.

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_BLE_RIF_ARB_COEX_PRI_ble_rx_pri

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_BLE_RIF_ARB_COEX_PRI_ble_rx_pri (   x)

ble_rx_pri - BLE Coex Priority, The BLE Rx packet high/medium priority that is used to make RIF_ARB arbitration request.

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_BLE_BCA_COEX_PRI_ble_tx_pri

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_BLE_BCA_COEX_PRI_ble_tx_pri (   x)

ble_tx_pri - BLE Coex Priority, The BLE Tx packet high/medium priority that is used to make BCA request.

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_BLE_BCA_COEX_PRI_ble_rx_pri

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_BLE_BCA_COEX_PRI_ble_rx_pri (   x)

ble_rx_pri - BLE Coex Priority, The BLE Rx packet high/medium priority that is used to make BCA request.

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_MWS_INACTIVITY_REFCLK_0_mwsinactmsg_refclk

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_MWS_INACTIVITY_REFCLK_0_mwsinactmsg_refclk (   x)

mwsinactmsg_refclk - Bluetooth Reference Clock Snapshot on MWS_Inact_Msg_Real_Int Interrupt, Contains the lower 16-bit of the reference clock[27:2] when the MWS_Inact_Msg_Real_Int interrupt pulse is detected.

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_MWS_INACTIVITY_REFCLK_1_mwsinactmsg_refclk

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_MWS_INACTIVITY_REFCLK_1_mwsinactmsg_refclk (   x)

mwsinactmsg_refclk - Bluetooth Reference Clock Snapshot on MWS_Inact_Msg_Real_Int Interrupt, Contains the upper 10-bit of the reference clock[27:2] when the MWS_Inact_Msg_Real_Int interrupt pulse is detected.

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_MWS_INACTIVITY_REFPDB_CNT_mwsinactmsg_pdbitcnt

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_MWS_INACTIVITY_REFPDB_CNT_mwsinactmsg_pdbitcnt (   x)

mwsinactmsg_pdbitcnt - Bluetooth Reference pdBitCounter Snapshot on MWS_Inact_Msg_Real_Int Interrupt, Contains the reference pdBitCounter[12:0] when the MWS_Inact_Msg_Real_Int interrupt pulse is detected.

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_INBAND_FREQ_SET_1_bt_in_band_lo

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_INBAND_FREQ_SET_1_bt_in_band_lo (   x)

bt_in_band_lo - Bluetooth In Band Low, In MRVL Coexistence mode; this is the low bound of the frequency channel for BT_FREQ.

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_INBAND_FREQ_SET_1_bt_in_band_hi

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_INBAND_FREQ_SET_1_bt_in_band_hi (   x)

bt_in_band_hi - Bluetooth In Band High, In MRVL Coexistence mode; this is the high bound of the frequency channel for BT_FREQ.

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_MODE_SELECT_bt_coex_mode

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_MODE_SELECT_bt_coex_mode (   x)

bt_coex_mode - Bluetooth Coexistence Mode Select, 0x0: disable, 0x1: CSR mode, 0x2: MRVL mode, 0x3: SUSHI mode

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_MODE_SELECT_bt_mws_coex_en

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_MODE_SELECT_bt_mws_coex_en (   x)

bt_mws_coex_en - Bluetooth and MWS Coexistence Mode Enable

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_MODE_SELECT_bt_ovlp_mode

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_MODE_SELECT_bt_ovlp_mode (   x)

bt_ovlp_mode - Bluetooth Overlap Mode, Select bt_overlap assertion mode, 0x0: non-filtered; old design, 0x1: filtered mode

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_MODE_SELECT_ovlp_supress_en

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_MODE_SELECT_ovlp_supress_en (   x)

ovlp_supress_en - Overlap Suppression Enable, Suppress BT_Overlap/BT_Scan_Overlap assertion if Tx power is less than the programmed threshold., 0x0: disable, 0x1: enable, BT_Overlap/BT_Scan_Overlap assertion to BCA will be suppressed if Tx_Power_Index, Value is less than the programmed BTU_PTA_TX_POWER_THRESHOLD for BR/EDR packet, type or is less than BTU_PTA_BLE_TX_PWR_THRHD for BLE packet.

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_MODE_SELECT_bt_scan_ovlp_mode

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_MODE_SELECT_bt_scan_ovlp_mode (   x)

bt_scan_ovlp_mode - Bluetooth Scan Overlap Mode, Select bt_scan_overlap assertion mode, 0x0: non-filtered; old design, 0x1: filtered mode

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_MODE_SELECT_bt_scan_ovlp_rx_en

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_MODE_SELECT_bt_scan_ovlp_rx_en (   x)

bt_scan_ovlp_rx_en - Scan Overlap for Bluetooth Rx, Enables bt_scan_overlap assertion for Bluetooth Rx., 0x0: disable, 0x1: enable

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_MODE_SELECT_bt_3dg_coex_en

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_MODE_SELECT_bt_3dg_coex_en (   x)

bt_3dg_coex_en - Bluetooth and 3D-Glass Coexistence Mode Enable

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_MODE_SELECT_bt_in_band1_freq_en

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_MODE_SELECT_bt_in_band1_freq_en (   x)

bt_in_band1_freq_en - BTU_PTA_INBAND_FREQ_SET_1 Band Gap Use Enable

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_MODE_SELECT_bt_tx_on_mode

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_MODE_SELECT_bt_tx_on_mode (   x)

bt_tx_on_mode - BT_TX_ON mode selection, Selects the mode of operation for asserting the BT_TX_ON signal., - Bit[15]: Direct Mode: BT_TX_ON signal assertion is independent of MWS Rx and MWS scan operations, - Bit[14]: Filtered Mode: BT_TX_ON signal is asserted in real time by hardware by taking into account of MWS Rx overlap and MWS scan overlap conditions

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_INBAND_FREQ_SET_0_bt_in_band_lo

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_INBAND_FREQ_SET_0_bt_in_band_lo (   x)

bt_in_band_lo - Bluetooth In Band Low, In MRVL Coexistence mode; this is the low bound of the frequency channel for BT_FREQ.

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_INBAND_FREQ_SET_0_bt_in_band_hi

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_INBAND_FREQ_SET_0_bt_in_band_hi (   x)

bt_in_band_hi - Bluetooth In Band High, In MRVL Coexistence mode; this is the high bound of the frequency channel for BT_FREQ.

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_HARQ_PATTERN_SEL_REFCLK_0_harqpatternsel_refclk

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_HARQ_PATTERN_SEL_REFCLK_0_harqpatternsel_refclk (   x)

harqpatternsel_refclk - Bluetooth Reference Clock Snapshot on HARQ_Pattern_Sel_Real_Int Interrupt, Contains the lower 16-bit of the referenceClock[27:2] when the HARQ_Pattern_Sel_Real_Int interrupt pulse is detected.

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_HARQ_PATTERN_SEL_REFCLK_1_harqpatternsel_refclk

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_HARQ_PATTERN_SEL_REFCLK_1_harqpatternsel_refclk (   x)

harqpatternsel_refclk - Bluetooth Reference Clock Snapshot on HARQ_Pattern_Sel_Real_Int Interrupt, Contains the upper 10-bit of the referenceClock[27:2] when the HARQ_Pattern_Sel_Real_Int interrupt pulse is detected.

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_HARQ_PATTERN_SEL_REFPDB_CNT_harqpatternsel_pdbitcnt

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_HARQ_PATTERN_SEL_REFPDB_CNT_harqpatternsel_pdbitcnt (   x)

harqpatternsel_pdbitcnt - Bluetooth Reference pdBitCounter Snapshot on HARQ_Pattern_Sel_Real_Int Interrupt, Contains the reference pdBitCounter[12:0] when the HARQ_Pattern_Sel_Real_Int interrupt pulse is detected.

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_HARQ_PATTERN_SEL_VAL_harqpatternselreg

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_HARQ_PATTERN_SEL_VAL_harqpatternselreg (   x)

harqpatternselreg - MWS Coex Signal HARQ_PATTERN_SEL Value, Contains the new HARQ_PATTERN_SEL value being received. When new HARQ_PATTERN_SEL is detected; the HARQ_Pattern_Sel_Real_Int real-time interrupt will be generated and new value of HARQ_PATTERN_SEL will be saved in here.

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_HARQ_PATTERN_SEL_VAL_mwsinactmsgreg

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_HARQ_PATTERN_SEL_VAL_mwsinactmsgreg (   x)

mwsinactmsgreg - MWS Coex Signal MWS_INACT_MSG Value, Contains the new MWS_INACT_MSG value being received. When new MWS_INACT_MSG is detected; the MWS_Inact_Msg_Real_Int real-time interrupt will be generated and new value of MWS_INACT_MSG will be saved here.

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_INFO_DELAY_TIMER_pta_info_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_INFO_DELAY_TIMER_pta_info_delay (   x)

pta_info_delay - Bluetooth Sniff Interval, When BT_Coex_Mode is set to MRVL mode; this is the time transition time for each of the PTA info bits (i.e.; priority bits) that are serially encoded on BT_STATE line., A value of 0 is invalid; and will lead to unknown results., - Unit: 0.25 us

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_BLE_SYNC_CTRL_ble_sync_priority

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_BLE_SYNC_CTRL_ble_sync_priority (   x)

ble_sync_priority - BLE Sync Priority, Sets Priority (sync_priority) for sync frame. The bt_state encodes if the current request is a sync frame or a regular frame. Each frame also has a 2-bit priority. BLE firmware sync_priority value if BLE controls the radio.

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_BLE_SYNC_CTRL_ble_sync_dir

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_BLE_SYNC_CTRL_ble_sync_dir (   x)

ble_sync_dir - BLE Sync Direction, Controls the direction of the sync frame (sync_dir). BLE firmware sync_dir value if BLE controls the radio., 0x0: Rx first, 0x1: Tx first

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_BLE_SYNC_CTRL_ble_sync_frame_fw_val

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_BLE_SYNC_CTRL_ble_sync_frame_fw_val (   x)

ble_sync_frame_fw_val - BLE Sync Frame Firmware Value, BLE firmware sync_frame value if BLE controls the radio.

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_BLE_SYNC_CTRL_ble_sync_frame_extend

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_BLE_SYNC_CTRL_ble_sync_frame_extend (   x)

ble_sync_frame_extend - BLE Sync Frame Extend, BLE firmware sync_frame value will be extended (kept asserted) if BLE keeps controlling the radio. However; sync_frame value will be reset if BLE ongoing event got cutoff or abort., 0x0: sync_frame value will be de-asserted at falling edge of bt_req, 0x1: sync_frame value will be extended

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_PRI_INC_EBRAM_ADDR_ebram_addr

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_PRI_INC_EBRAM_ADDR_ebram_addr (   x)

ebram_addr - EBRAM Address

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_SYNC_CTRL_sync_mode_en

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_SYNC_CTRL_sync_mode_en (   x)

sync_mode_en - Sync Mode Enable, Enable MRVL Coexistence syncModel mode. The BT_STATE encodes if the current request is a sync frame or a regular frame. Each frame also has a 2-bit sync frame priority and a sync frame direction bit., If MRVL Coexistence syncModel mode is on; 4 more sync frame coexistence info are encoded sequentially on BT_STATE before other coexistence info:, 1. Sync Frame (sync_frame), 2. Sync Priority[0] (sync_priority[0]), 3. Sync Priority[1] (sync_priority[1]), 4. Sync Direction (sync_dir)

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_BLE_TX_PWR_THRHD_ble1m_tx_pwr_thrshld

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_BLE_TX_PWR_THRHD_ble1m_tx_pwr_thrshld (   x)

ble1m_tx_pwr_thrshld - BLE Tx power threshold for 1 Mbps packet to suppress BT_Overlap/BT_Scan_Overlap assertion

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_BLE_TX_PWR_THRHD_ble2m_tx_pwr_thrshld

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_BLE_TX_PWR_THRHD_ble2m_tx_pwr_thrshld (   x)

ble2m_tx_pwr_thrshld - BLE Tx power threshold for 2 Mbps packet to suppress BT_Overlap/BT_Scan_Overlap assertion

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_STATE_DELAY_TIMER_pta_state_delay

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_STATE_DELAY_TIMER_pta_state_delay (   x)

pta_state_delay - PTA State Delay, When BT_Coex_Mode is in MRVL mode; this controls the delay for the BT_STATE transition for the second transaction., This value should never be programmed less than 15us; otherwise unexpected behavior will occur; such as dropped packets., - Unit: 0.25 us.

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_SLNA_CTRL_ble_use_slna

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_SLNA_CTRL_ble_use_slna (   x)

ble_use_slna - BLE uses SLNA based on RSSI info, Among all Bluetooth link RSSI average information; software decides which path BLE traffic will use for Rx; for example; SLNA or path 3 of Bluetooth only., 0x0: use path 3 for Bluetooth only based on BLE RSSI average, 0x1: enable SLNA path based on BLE RSSI average

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_SLNA_CTRL_ble_slna_cp_en_bp_sel

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_SLNA_CTRL_ble_slna_cp_en_bp_sel (   x)

ble_slna_cp_en_bp_sel - BLE side enable SLNA bias/charge pump bypass for software control, By default; slna_cp_en is dynamically control by rifRx because it requires 120us in advance. However; software can bypass it to control this enable by ble_slna_cp_en_bp_val with this selection bit is set., 0x0: use hardware dynamically controlled SLNA_cp_en, 0x1: software bypass to control the SLNA_cp_en

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_SLNA_CTRL_ble_slna_cp_en_bp_val

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_SLNA_CTRL_ble_slna_cp_en_bp_val (   x)

ble_slna_cp_en_bp_val - SLNA_cp Enable, When ble_slna_cp_en_by_sel is set; this value controls if SLNA_cp is enabled., When ble_slna_cp_en_by_sel is set; software controls SLNA_cp_en to enable or disable by this bit., 0x0: eisable SLNA cp, 0x1: enable SLNA cp

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_TX_POWER_THRESHOLD_bdr_tx_pwr_thrshld

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_TX_POWER_THRESHOLD_bdr_tx_pwr_thrshld (   x)

bdr_tx_pwr_thrshld - Tx power threshold for BDR packet to suppress bt_overlap/bt_scan_overlap assertion

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_TX_POWER_THRESHOLD_edr_tx_pwr_thrshld

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_TX_POWER_THRESHOLD_edr_tx_pwr_thrshld (   x)

edr_tx_pwr_thrshld - Tx power threshold for EDR packet to suppress bt_overlap/bt_scan_overlap assertion

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_MWS_TX_OVERLAP_RANGE_tx_mws_overlap_range

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_MWS_TX_OVERLAP_RANGE_tx_mws_overlap_range (   x)

tx_mws_overlap_range - MWS frequency overlap range for BR/EDR Tx Slots, Under MWS Coex Mode; MWS frequency overlap range for BR/EDR Tx slots., Bit[7] direction bit:, - Bits[6:0] value: overlap boundary channel, The overlap boundary indexes the Bluetooth's 0~78 channels. The direction bit being 0 represents the upper boundary channel and all smaller index channels thereof are fallen in the overlap range; whereas the bit begin 1 represents the lower boundary channel and all larger index channels thereof are fallen in the overlap range., 0x0: upper boundary, 0x1: lower boundary

◆ BTU2_BTU_PTA_MWS_TX_OVERLAP_RANGE_ble_tx_mws_overlap_range

#define BTU2_BTU_PTA_MWS_TX_OVERLAP_RANGE_ble_tx_mws_overlap_range (   x)

ble_tx_mws_overlap_range - MWS frequency overlap range for BLE Tx packets, Under MWS Coex Mode; MWS frequency overlap range for BLE Tx packets., Bit[7] direction bit:, - Bits[6:0] value: overlap boundary channel, The overlap boundary indexes the Bluetooth's 0~78 channels.
